title: Prompt Injection, Tool Output Trust, Exfiltration, and Safe Rendering · Contract Workshop
summary: A public-interest product team incrementally builds instruction-data separation and agent security gateway from original fixed provider, interaction, tool, approval, evaluation, and failure fixtures while retaining prior Python, HTTP, prompt, loop, security, accessibility, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of instruction-data separation and agent security gateway while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through predictio…
objectives: Represent application policy, user instructions, retrieved data, tool output, memory, and provider metadata with explicit authority and non-executable boundaries.
Test direct and indirect goal hijacking, authority spoofing, hidden text, encoded payloads, multimodal instructions, and tool-result injection.
Treat tool names, content, links, files, citations, metadata, error text, and nested model output as untrusted typed data.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Instruction Data Separation
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop agent engineering team handles case 6633 during a clean package, canary, and recovery drill. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate instructions from data, resist direct and indirect injection, constrain tools, prevent exfiltration, and encode rendered output; telemetry and memory must remain tenant-isolated, minimized, redacted, and attributable; then change one eval case, rubric, model version, token price, rate limit, or latency distribution and rerun release gates. Browser code uses pure bounded state machines and original fixed fixtures only. Real provider APIs, Gemini models, google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic native validation, HTTP, files, credentials, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, privacy review, and production effects require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Represent application policy, user instructions, retrieved data, tool output, memory, and provider metadata with explicit authority and non-executable boundaries.

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Safe Output Rendering
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: agentpy-safe-output-rendering-ppsgyl" that uses case 6538 to separate instructions from data, resist direct and indirect injection, constrain tools, prevent exfiltration, and encode rendered output. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not contact provider APIs, invoke Gemini models, import or claim native google-genai, google-adk, or Pydantic behavior, open sockets, read or write host files, use credentials, execute generated code, launch browsers, reach databases or telemetry backends, or claim live privacy, load, safety, cost, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, google-genai 2.11.0, google-adk 2.4.0, Pydantic 2.13.4, fixed provider fixtures, fake transports, controlled local services, disposable cloud projects, representative eval sets, accessibility, privacy and security review, load, canary, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Safe AI agents and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Safe Output Rendering.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Safe Output Rendering into observable performance evidence. For Safe AI agents and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Safe Output Rendering, keep Safe Output Rendering connected to executable Python model, interaction, tool, policy, state, approval, evaluation, security, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-safe-output-rendering-ppsgyl
# Competency: agentpy-safe-output-rendering.
# Case 6538: an accessible public-service intake assistant.
# Operating constraint: every external effect requires explicit least-authority policy.
# Changed case: change one eval case, rubric, model version, token price, rate limit, or latency distribution and rerun release gates.
def evidence_agentpy_safe_output_rendering_ppsgyl(untrusted_inputs: int = 40, rejected_instructions: int = 18, admitted_data: int = 22, exfiltration_events: int = 0, unsafe_renderings: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_ppsgyl = "ppsgyl-lygspp-6538"
    assert evidence_variant_ppsgyl
    evidence_axes_ppsgyl = (
        "authorityppsgyl",
        "interaction6538",
        "toollygspp",
        "stateppsgyl6538",
        "evaluation6538ppsgyl",
        "recoverylygspp6538",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_ppsgyl)) == 6
    if untrusted_inputs < 1 or rejected_instructions < 0 or admitted_data < 0:
        return False, untrusted_inputs, "security-input-count"
    if rejected_instructions + admitted_data != untrusted_inputs:
        return False, rejected_instructions, "instruction-data-reconciliation"
    if exfiltration_events > 0:
        return False, exfiltration_events, "data-exfiltration"
    if unsafe_renderings > 0:
        return False, unsafe_renderings, "unsafe-output-rendering"
    assert rejected_instructions + admitted_data == untrusted_inputs and exfiltration_events == 0
    return True, rejected_instructions, "secured-agent-boundary"
